entities.raw_quote: the prince of these demons was Beelzebub or Satan.

entities.name: Satan
entities.raw_quote: the prince of these demons was Beelzebub or Satan.

entities.name: daimonia

attributes.role: evil spirits, messengers/angels of the prince of demons

entities.raw_quote: the daimonia of Scripture, evil spirits, the messengers or angels of the prince of the demons

entities.name: Hermes Trismegistus

attributes.role: supposed originator of ancient Pagan magic

entities.raw_quote: The spirits invoked by the necromancers and the magicians were supposed to be the spirits of the dead, and this may have been the belief of the first great magician, Hermes himself.

entities.name: Clementine Homilies
entities.raw_quote: Clement, who is supposed to be the person of that name mentioned by the Apostle Paul...

entities.name: necromancy

attributes.definition: art of calling up souls from Hades
attributes.purpose: consultation with spirits of the dead

entities.raw_quote: induce him by the offer of a large sum of money to call up a soul from Hades, by the art which is termed necromancy

entities.name: hierophauts and prophets of the shrines
entities.raw_quote: I will go to Egypt and cultivate the friendship of the hierophauts and prophets of the shrines

entities.name: Jamblicus
entities.raw_quote: the disciples of Jamblicus asserted that they had often witnessed the same miracle in the case of their master, who when he prayed was raised to the height of ten cubits from the earth

entities.name: Zoroaster (Chaldean)
entities.raw_quote: luminous rays emanating from the soul do sometimes divinely penetrate the body, which is then of itself raised above the earth, and that this was the case with Zoroaster

entities.name: Francis of Assisi
entities.raw_quote: the so-called 'saints,' Francis of Assisi, Petrus a Martina, and Francis of Macerata... raised from the earth when he prayed

entities.name: Francis of Macerata
entities.raw_quote: his body became luminous, 'a flame resting on his head'

entities.name: St Philip Neri
entities.raw_quote: Philip was often seen with his whole body raised in the air... when he was saying Mass

entities.name: Hecate
entities.raw_quote: in the fumes of the incense which he burned before the statue of 'Hecate,' the image was seen to laugh

entities.name: Isis
entities.raw_quote: the statue of Isis shook the silver serpent on her forehead and nodded assent to her worshippers

entities.name: Theurgists
entities.raw_quote: The Theurgists caused the appearance of the gods (i.e., daimonia) in the air in the midst of a gaseous vapour

entities.name: Psellus
entities.raw_quote: when the priests used their magical powers, 'the statues laughed and lamps were spontaneously kindled'

entities.name: Lucan
entities.raw_quote: Lucan... speaks of the images of the gods weeping as foretelling misfortune

entities.name: Virgil
entities.raw_quote: Virgil... Georgics... tears shed by Gods our country's patrons / And sweat from Lares told the city's woes

entities.name: Spiritualism (19th c.)
entities.raw_quote: The revival in the nineteenth century of the long-disused practices of necromancy is a startling fact

entities.name: Wallace (naturalist)
entities.raw_quote: My position, therefore, is that the phenomena of Spiritualism in their entirety do not require further confirmation

entities.name: Clementine Homilies (source)
entities.raw_quote: a passage from the Clementine Homilies, quoted by Mr Pember, very clearly shows what was generally believed at the time they were written

summary: This chunk argues that spirit communication practices—including ancient necromancy, pagan theurgy, Christian saint veneration, and modern Spiritualism—are all manifestations of the same underlying phenomenon: communication with evil spirits (daimonia) rather than departed souls. It traces historical continuity from Hermes Trismegistus through late antique Theurgists to medieval Catholic saints and 19th-century Spiritualism, identifying consistent patterns of levitation, materialization, clairvoyance, and spirit manifestation as evidence of demonic agency.
key_passages: the spirits who reply to the deluded votaries of Spiritualism... are not the spirits of the dead, but identical in every respect with the daimonia of Scripture, and the demon gods of the Pagans.
Clement... induce him by the offer of a large sum of money to call up a soul from Hades, by the art which is termed necromancy...
The whole system, in short, of the ancient Paganism was professedly the worship of the dead.
the spirits invoked are supposed to be, and claim themselves to be, the spirits of departed relatives or friends... This was the character of the magic taught by Hermes Trismegistus.
My position, therefore, is that the phenomena of Spiritualism in their entirety do not require further confirmation. They are proved quite as well as any facts are proved in other sciences.
